children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: a Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, for the activities described in subsection (b), $2,130,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.label: i
children.text: entering into contracts, including stewardship contracts or agreements, the purpose of each of which shall be to restore ecological health on not fewer than 10,000 acres of Federal land, including Indian forest land or rangeland, and for salaries and expenses associated with preparing and executing those contracts; and

children.label: ii
children.text: establishing a Working Capital Fund that may be accessed by the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Agriculture to fund requirements of contracts described in clause (i), including cancellation and termination costs, consistent with , and periodic payments over the span of the contract period; andsection 6591c(h) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: $50,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of the Interior to enter into contracts described in subparagraph (A)(i);

children.label: ii
children.text: $150,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture to enter into contracts described in subparagraph (A)(i); and

children.label: iii
children.text: $100,000,000 shall be made available until expended to the Secretary of the Interior, notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, to establish the Working Capital Fund described in subparagraph (A)(ii);

children.label: A
children.text: $40,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: B
children.text: $160,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture;

$400,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture to provide financial assistance to facilities that purchase and process byproducts from ecosystem restoration projects in accordance with subsection (d);

children.label: A
children.text: prioritizes funding cross-boundary projects; and

children.label: B
children.text: requires matching funding from the State, territory of the United States, or Indian Tribe to be eligible to receive the funding;

$50,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture to award grants to States and Indian Tribes to establish rental programs for portable skidder bridges, bridge mats, or other temporary water crossing structures, to minimize stream bed disturbance on non-Federal land and Federal land;

children.label: A
children.text: $100,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: B
children.text: $100,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture;
$100,000,000 shall be made available to restore, prepare, or adapt recreation sites on Federal land, including Indian forest land or rangeland, in accordance with subsection (e);

children.label: A
children.text: $100,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: B
children.text: $100,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture;

children.label: A
children.text: $70,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: B
children.text: $130,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture; and

$80,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture, in coordination with the Secretary of the Interior, to establish a collaborative-based, landscape-scale restoration program to restore water quality or fish passage on Federal land, including Indian forest land or rangeland, in accordance with subsection (f).

children.heading: Submission of list of projects to congress
children.label: 1 Submission of list of projects to congress
A
children.text: a detailed description of each project; and

children.label: B
children.text: an estimate of the cost, including salaries and expenses, for the project.

children.heading: Alternate allocation
children.label: 2 Alternate allocation
children.text: Appropriations Acts may provide for alternate allocation of amounts made available under subsection (b)(1), consistent with the allocations under subparagraph (B) of that subsection.

children.heading: Lack of alternate allocations
children.label: 3 Lack of alternate allocations
children.text: If Congress has not enacted legislation establishing alternate allocations described in paragraph (2) by the date on which the Act making full-year appropriations for the Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies for the applicable fiscal year is enacted into law, amounts made available under subsection (b)(1)(B) shall be allocated by the President.

children.label: A
children.text: very low priority for ecological restoration involving vegetation removal;

children.label: B
children.text: low priority for ecological restoration involving vegetation removal;

children.label: C
children.text: medium priority for ecological restoration involving vegetation removal;

children.label: D
children.text: high priority for ecological restoration involving vegetation removal; or

children.label: E
children.text: very high priority for ecological restoration involving vegetation removal;

children.label: A
children.text: a sawmill or other wood-processing facility exists in close proximity to, or a forest worker is seeking to conduct restoration treatment work on or in close proximity to, the unit; and

children.label: B
children.text: the presence of a sawmill or other wood-processing facility would substantially decrease or does substantially decrease the cost of conducting ecological restoration projects involving vegetation removal;

in accordance with any conditions the Secretary of Agriculture determines to be necessary, using the amounts made available under subsection (b)(3), provide financial assistance, including a low-interest loan or a loan guarantee, to an entity seeking to establish, reopen, retrofit, expand, or improve a sawmill or other wood-processing facility in close proximity to a unit of Federal land that has been identified under paragraph (1) as high or very high priority for ecological restoration, if the presence of a sawmill or other wood-processing facility would substantially decrease or does substantially decrease the cost of conducting ecological restoration projects involving vegetation removal on the unit of Federal land, including Indian forest land or rangeland, as determined under paragraph (2)(B); and

children.label: A
children.text: has been identified under paragraph (1) as being high or very high priority for ecological restoration involving vegetation removal; and

children.label: I
children.text: exists in close proximity to the unit; and

children.label: II
children.text: does substantially decrease the cost of conducting ecological restoration projects involving vegetation removal on the unit; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that has received financial assistance under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Site restoration and improvements
children.label: 1 Site restoration and improvements
children.text: Of the amounts made available under subsection (b)(7), $45,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of the Interior and $35,000,000 shall be made available the Secretary of Agriculture to restore, prepare, or adapt recreation sites on Federal land, including Indian forest land or rangeland, that have experienced or may likely experience visitation and use beyond the carrying capacity of the sites.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “to”.1

children.label: i
children.text: the operation, repair, reconstruction, and construction of public use recreation cabins on National Forest System land; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent necessary, the repair or reconstruction of historic buildings that are to be outleased under .section 306121 of title 54

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: B Inclusion
children.text: Of the amount described in subparagraph (A), $5,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of Agriculture for associated salaries and expenses in carrying out that subparagraph.

children.label: i
children.text: to carry out the activities described in subparagraph (A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: to manage the renting of a cabin or building described in subparagraph (A) to the public.

children.label: A
children.text: funding for the project would be used for deferred maintenance, as defined by Federal Accounting Standards Advisory Board; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Agriculture has identified the project for funding from the National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund established by .section 200402(a) of title 54

children.heading: Collaborative-based, aquatic-focused, landscape-scale restoration program
children.label: f Collaborative-based, aquatic-focused, landscape-scale restoration program
A
children.text: are for 5-year projects to restore fish passage or water quality on Federal land and non-Federal land to the extent allowed under , including Indian forest land or rangeland;section 1011a(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: contain proposed accomplishments and proposed non-Federal funding; and

children.label: C
children.text: request not more than $5,000,000 in funding made available under subsection (b)(10);

children.label: A
children.text: gives priority to a project proposal that would result in the most miles of streams being restored for the lowest amount of Federal funding; and

children.label: B
children.text: discontinues funding for a project that fails to achieve the results included in a proposal submitted under paragraph (1) for more than 2 consecutive years; and

children.label: A
children.text: all of the priority watersheds on National Forest System land;

children.label: B
children.text: the condition of each priority watershed on ; andNovember 15, 2021

children.label: C
children.text: the condition of each priority watershed on the date that is 5 years after .November 15, 2021
